headline: China factory activity beats on tech export demand
detail: June manufacturing data came in stronger than expected, driven specifically by tech export orders. This is a direct positive catalyst for semiconductor and chip-equipment names — your SMH, SOXL, and CRWD-adjacent tech exposure all benefit from improved demand signals out of China's supply chain.

headline: U.S.-Iran deal sends oil above $70
detail: An agreement to halt attacks has stabilized oil prices in the low $70s. Reduced geopolitical risk supports risk-on sentiment, which helps your growth and tech-heavy book. Stable energy costs also protect data center and hyperscaler capex plans that underpin chip demand.

headline: Trump Iran strikes historically spark Monday rallies
detail: The data pattern cited shows that post-Iran-strike Mondays have tended to produce stock market gains. Combined with the deal news, this reinforces today's cautiously bullish tone — particularly relevant for momentum names like CRWD where sentiment momentum matters as much as fundamentals.

headline: U.S.-China tech rivalry spreading to third countries
detail: The rivalry is now playing out in emerging and allied markets, not just bilateral trade. This creates both risk (further export restrictions) and opportunity (domestic chip production push) for your semiconductor holdings like SMH and SOXL — worth monitoring for any new restriction announcements.
